lesson.menu_desc: Hotel requests & complaints, reporting questions and requests -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- The Perfect Stay: Hotels
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Pricila Adamo | Aula 7 -- The Perfect Stay: Hotels

listenings.text: Good evening, this is a message from the front desk for the guest in room four-one-two. Earlier you asked whether late check-out was possible, and I am pleased to confirm that we can offer it until two o'clock at no extra charge. You also asked the concierge to arrange a taxi to the airport; that is now booked for half past two. Finally, regarding the noise you reported last night, the manager has asked me to apologise and has upgraded your room to a quiet suite on the top floor for your final evening. Please do let us know if there is anything else we can do.

Honestly, that hotel turned a tricky night into a lovely stay. When I checked in, the receptionist asked whether I had stayed with them before, and she told me to keep the welcome drink voucher for the rooftop bar. There was a small discrepancy on my bill, so I asked the manager to look into it, and within minutes he had removed the charge and added a courtesy breakfast. I learned something on this trip: if you stay calm and explain the problem clearly, most hotels will go out of their way to accommodate you. A good hotel is not the one where nothing goes wrong, but the one that puts it right.
